title: Python Consumable Contract Extraction
goal: Resolve #320 by extracting stable authority marker policy values out of cli.py into a checked-in contract and schema.
Advance #258 by making Python consume that contract while preserving runtime path normalization and matching behavior.
non_goals: Do not rewrite ownership ledger matching.
Do not move dynamic path normalization or matching order out of Python.
Do not close #258 unless the broader declarative portability parent is demonstrably complete.

intent.outcome: Authority marker static values are authored in a contract, validated by schema/tooling, and consumed by cli.py.
intent.constraints: Keep runtime path normalization and path classification procedural; preserve start, implement, ownership --path, and llms.txt behavior.
intent.latitude: Choose contract file shape, schema names, and representative parity tests.
intent.escalation: Stop if extraction requires changing marker semantics or broad ownership matching.

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan, commit, push, and close #320/#258.
execution.proof: Contract tooling validates the new contract and tests prove existing marker outputs stay stable.

invariants: Marker policy static payloads are contract-owned.
Existing start, implement, ownership --path, and llms.txt marker outputs remain compatible.

required_continuation.activation trigger: authority marker values remain authored only in Python or #258 still has stable extractable Python-owned contract truth

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Non-Python adapters can consume authority marker policy data without reverse-engineering cli.py.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Further extraction of procedural lifecycle/runtime behavior not owned by #320.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The extraction map needed an explicit extracted state so completed slices no longer look pending.

intent_interpretation.literal request: execute all planned work lane by lane and dogfood the package
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: complete the next planned extraction slice without collapsing procedural runtime behavior into static data
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: extract authority marker static policy data to contract JSON and consume it from Python
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject work that changes marker semantics, broadens ownership matching, or closes #258 without evidence

execution_bounds.allowed paths: authority marker contract/schema, contract inventory/extraction map, cli marker consumer, contract tooling checker, focused tests, active planning state and execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "authority_marker or start or implement"; uv run agentic-workspace start --format json; uv run agentic-workspace implement --changed AGENTS.md --format json; uv run agentic-workspace ownership --target . --path llms.txt --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing marker semantics, parser surfaces, or ownership ledger matching

stop_conditions.stop when: #320 is shipped, dogfooded, committed, pushed, and closed or residual work is routed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: contract data cannot express current marker values without changing runtime path behavior
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: implementation expands into ownership ledger or lifecycle extraction

context_budget.live working set: #320, #258, authority marker code/tests, contract tooling checker, active execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full declarative portability history and closed extraction issues
context_budget.externalize before shift: update this execplan execution_run and immediate_next_action
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace summary and gh issue view #320/#258
context_budget.tiny resumability note: extract stable authority marker policy values, keep matching procedural
context_budget.context-shift triggers: authority marker extraction implies broader ownership matching changes

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ship Python-consumable authority marker policy extraction
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: preserve behavior; keep dynamic path matching in Python; schema-validate the contract
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: contract shape and representative parity tests
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the broader #258 parent would need multiple further extraction slices

active_milestone.scope: contract/schema plus Python consumer and parity proof

contract_decisions_to_freeze: Authority marker payload data is stable policy data; selector normalization and matching stay procedural.
open_questions_to_close: Closed: authority_markers.json stores static marker payload data plus representative paths; Python keeps path normalization and rule matching.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "authority_marker or start or implement"
uv run agentic-workspace implement --changed AGENTS.md --format json
uv run agentic-workspace ownership --target . --path llms.txt --format json
completion_criteria: Authority marker static values are no longer authored only inside cli.py.
The contract and schema are validated by contract tooling.
Representative paths prove each marker rule can produce the expected payload.
Existing start, implement, ownership --path, and llms.txt behavior remains compatible.

execution_run.what happened: Added authority_markers.json and schema, wired cli.py authority marker payloads to consume the validated contract, added representative-path parity checks, updated contract inventory/consumption metadata, and gave python_extraction_map an extracted state for completed slices.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace contract data/schema/tooling, cli authority marker consumer, focused CLI tests, extraction map, and active planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "authority_marker or start or implement"; uv run ruff check src scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py; uv run agentic-workspace start --format json; uv run agentic-workspace implement --changed AGENTS.md --format json; uv run agentic-workspace ownership --target . --path llms.txt --format json; uv run agentic-workspace ownership --target . --path src/agentic_workspace/contracts/authority_markers.json --format json; uv run pytest -q; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #320 is complete and #258's planned extraction blocker is closed; continue with further-product-compression-on-repeated-pressure.

proof_report.validation proof: Contract tooling, focused tests, full pytest, ruff, maintainer surfaces, live start/implement/ownership dogfood, summary, planning checker, and diff check passed; diff check only reported the existing state.toml line-ending warning.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: authority_markers.json is schema-validated and consumed by cli.py; representative-path parity covers every marker rule; live start/implement/ownership outputs preserve AGENTS.md, llms.txt, planning, and source marker behavior.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Extract authority marker policy data from cli.py.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Authority labels, canonical source policy, safe-to-edit defaults, refresh commands, and representative paths now live in authority_markers.json with schema and parity checks; cli.py keeps only path normalization and matching behavior.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep durable, shareable contract truth in small checked-in surfaces and use Python as a reference implementation where possible.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Extract stable policy data only; keep live runtime behavior procedural.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make authority marker behavior consumable by non-Python adapters without reverse-engineering cli.py?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #320's concrete marker extraction landed and #258's planned portability lane no longer has a known stable Python-owned contract blocker; remaining runtime and template candidates are either extracted, classified, deferred with reason, or owned by later roadmap lanes.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan proof_report plus authority_markers contract/schema, contract tooling parity, live dogfood outputs, and python_extraction_map extracted/deferred classifications
closure_check.reopen trigger: authority marker values remain Python-authored or marker outputs drift

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Authority marker static policy data is contract-backed and Python consumes it as a reference adapter while keeping runtime matching procedural.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: contract tooling, focused tests, full pytest, ruff, maintainer surfaces, live dogfood commands, planning checker, and diff check
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: contract inventory, python_contract_consumption, and python_extraction_map updated
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Contract metadata updated; no Memory changes needed.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted python-consumable contract extraction from roadmap, with #320 as the first slice and #258 as parent.
